title: [skip-ci] Packit: remove epel and re-enable c9s
body:
We're moving away from proper rhel testing on upstream because of the slower pace of RHEL. This has already been done on aardvark-dns and some others.

CentOS 9 Stream does move fast enough that we can re-enable it here.

<!--

a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Cockpit tests failed for commit 415d762475126282ca9d650a4a94e7e7435d193e. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
@martinpitt @jelly something's up with cockpit on rawhide, doesn't seem related to the PR though.

@lsm5 indeed, it seems criu or crun broke today, see the [error message in the screenshot](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/4ff7f22a-1bc3-4c0b-944c-ff705a67615d/work-podman-miscrmewa1yc/plans/cockpit-podman/podman-misc/execute/data/guest/default-0/test/browser/other-1/data/TestApplication-testCheckpointRestore-fedora-42-localhost-22-FAIL-1.png)

The [criu log](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/4ff7f22a-1bc3-4c0b-944c-ff705a67615d/work-podman-miscrmewa1yc/plans/cockpit-podman/podman-misc/execute/data/guest/default-0/test/browser/other-1/data/TestApplication-testCheckpointRestore-dump.log) shows some details:

```
(00.043508) Error (criu/vdso.c:381): vdso: Unexpected rt vDSO area bounds
(00.043513) Error (criu/vdso.c:613): vdso: Failed to fill self vdso symtable
(00.043518) Error (criu/kerndat.c:1906): kerndat_vdso_fill_symtable failed when initializing kerndat.
```

[criu 4.0-2](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-8154a1b7a3) is already a month old, and [crun 1.18.2-1](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-6ad54ee43a) 3 weeks. New kernel/toolchain?

I'll download a rawhide VM and investigate. 

However, I wonder, don't you have any coverage of `podman container checkpoint/restore` in podman's own tests? This does break fairly often, e.g. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24230 or https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2105220 or https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1944964 or https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-58354

I reported the criu/kernel regression to https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2328985 and silenced it in our tests in https://github.com/cockpit-project/bots/pull/7146 . From now on, this failure will be ignored, i.e. tests should go back to green.

> However, I wonder, don't you have any coverage of podman container checkpoint/restore in podman's own tests?

We have plenty of checkpoint tests but we do not update our CI images so often so we lack behind. And we really cannot do these `dnf install/update` in a PR as this just constantly breaks for all kind of reasons. And then https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24230 came exactly from such image update PR that broke in our own tests so we very much catch them (at some point) but if they do not get fixed then there is nothing we can do about it. So we skip and move on.


 

> I reported the criu/kernel regression to https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2328985 and silenced it in our tests in [cockpit-project/bots#7146](https://github.com/cockpit-project/bots/pull/7146) . From now on, this failure will be ignored, i.e. tests should go back to green.

Thanks

@lsm5 ah, thanks! That's very sensible indeed, we do the same for cockpit (and then these "image refreshes" expose all the OS regressions). I keep forgetting that you don't run podman tests in tmt/testing farm. Anyway, I think all good now.

@lsm5: new pull request created: #24710

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24682#issuecomment-2507657014):
